data.text: strong affection for another arising out of kinship or personal ties
examples: maternal love for a child
… share their love and support for one another …
people searching for love
… asking couples to share videos capturing the essence of love and romance …
her love for her best friend
The coach earned the love and respect of his team.
give her my love
Animal lovers … responded …, sending love and donations to the rescue canine.
their love of the sea
… bet big on the public's love of women's sports.
Baseball was his first love.
his lady love
… a music video … which shows the artist spending a day alone, enjoying her own company while thinking about her love.
divine love
… he saw the whole world forming one vast symmetrical expression of God's power and love.
brotherly love
their secret love
making love
conjugal love

data.text: to feel great affection for: to hold dear: cherish
examples: You know how much I love my family.
… it would be an understatement to say people really love their pets.
He loves her, but she doesn't love him back.
He loved to play the violin. = He loved playing the violin.
"Would you like to join us?" "Yes, I'd love to."
I don't love this color on me.
… shoppers love how versatile the dress is.
I just love it when politicians say that they don't care about polls.
The rosebush loves sunlight.
'Tis Better to have loved and lost / Than never to have loved at all.

data.text: a romantic attachment or episode between lovers
examples: America's love affair with baseball
America's love affair with baseball

data.text: beads worn as a symbol of love and peace
a child whose parents were not married when the child was conceived; especially: the child of an affair —often used figuratively
examples: My older sister, Jean Marie, was the love child of an affair my mother had with a married man who, instead of leaving his wife for my mother, died of a heart attack.
The museum is the love child of the company's owner, Jacques Lorch, who for twenty-five years has been ferreting out artifacts pertaining to the subject that is his passion.

data.text: something (such as a project or collaboration) that is a product of hard work and devotion
examples: My older sister, Jean Marie, was the love child of an affair my mother had with a married man who, instead of leaving his wife for my mother, died of a heart attack.
The museum is the love child of the company's owner, Jacques Lorch, who for twenty-five years has been ferreting out artifacts pertaining to the subject that is his passion.

data.text: a meal eaten in common by a Christian congregation in token of brotherly love
a gathering held to promote reconciliation and good feeling or show someone affectionate honor
any of a genus (Eragrostis) of grasses that resemble the bluegrasses but have flattened spikelets and deciduous lemmas
fatty bulges along the sides of the body at the waist
a gathering of people especially for the expression of their mutual love

enrichment.etymology: Middle English, from Old English lufu; akin to Old High German luba love, Old English lēof dear, Latin lubēre, libēre to please
